task_completion.description: Divide the space into 4 zones using partition walls and at least 4 doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan or 3D view for interior walls that create at least 4 distinct enclosed or semi-enclosed areas, with at least 4 doors placed within these walls.

task_completion.description: Define the rooms and label at least three zones on the floor plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan for room objects (indicated by floor area shading or square footage measurements) and text labels for at least three of the required zones (Main Dispatch Floor, Secure IT/Server Room, Supervisor Office, Staff Break/Nap Room).

task_completion.description: Equip the IT/Server Room with distinct anti-static flooring and at least 4 large shelving units or cabinets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a room with a floor color or texture that is noticeably different from the rest of the building, containing at least 4 shelving units or cabinets (representing server racks).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Staff Break/Nap Room with at least 2 beds or sofas and at least 2 kitchen appliances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a room containing at least 2 beds or sofas and at least 2 kitchen appliances (such as a refrigerator, microwave, or oven).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Main Dispatch Floor with at least 8 consoles (large desks or tables) and 8 ergonomic chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a large area containing at least 8 large desks or tables and at least 8 chairs (typically office or ergonomic style) arranged as dispatch consoles.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window open with the project being actively edited, rather than a text editor modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used only the built-in furniture catalog.
integrity.visual_evidence: The placed items should visually correspond to standard Sweet Home 3D default assets from the left-hand catalog panel. There should be no obvious imported custom 3D models or external asset download windows.

integrity.description: The agent saved the project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar should show the file name without an asterisk (*), or there should be a screenshot showing the save dialog or terminal confirming the save action.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use Sweet Home 3D to edit 'dispatch_starter.sh3d'. The total score is 100 points, with a pass threshold of 70. The scoring is divided into 5 components: 20 points for walls/doors (4 zones), 20 points for room definitions/labels (at least 3 labels), 15 points for the IT/Server room (distinct flooring, >=4 shelves/cabinets), 15 points for the Break Room (>=2 beds/sofas, >=2 kitchen appliances), and 30 points for the Dispatch Floor (>=8 desks/tables, >=8 chairs).
